channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australia Considers Regulating Big Tech to Improve Customer Service
item.description: Australian authorities are exploring new ways to improve accountability among major tech companies such as Meta, Apple, Google, and Microsoft, whose platforms have become critical to daily life for in...

item.title: Focusing on Whole-Body Wellness Beyond the Scale
item.description: For decades, women have been taught to equate their health and self-worth with the numbers on a scale. The obsession with weight has often turned these numbers into a judgement of discipline, attracti...

item.title: EFF leader Malema's defence seeks non-custodial sentence in firearm case
item.description: EFF leader Julius Malema's defence team is requesting a non-custodial sentence following his conviction on five charges, including unlawful possession and discharge of a firearm at a 2018 rally in Mda...

item.title: Finance Minister Aurangzeb Discusses Pakistan’s Economic Challenges Amid Middle East Conflict in Washington
item.description: Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb visited Washington to participate in the World Bank and IMF Spring Meetings 2026, engaging in over 50 high-level meetings. During his visit, he met US Commerce Secr...

item.title: HONOR announces the forthcoming HONOR 600 Lite with AI-powered photography and durable design
item.description: Global AI device maker HONOR has officially announced that the HONOR 600 Lite will soon launch in South Africa, promising a stylish, high-performance smartphone crafted for everyday use. The device br...

item.title: Government cancels 40 inactive mining licences to promote investment and discipline
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has withdrawn 40 mining exploration licences that had remained unused, in an effort to encourage discipline, promote active investment, and stop malpractice in the extractiv...

item.title: Embakasi Matatu 'Optimus Prime' Involved in Serious Head-On Collision on Jogoo Road
item.description: A well-known Embakasi matatu named 'Optimus Prime' was involved in a serious head-on collision with a smaller white 14-seater PSV along the busy Jogoo Road in Nairobi. Videos circulating online show t...

item.title: Government Applies Highest Subsidy to Keep Kerosene Prices Stable Despite Global Oil Surge
item.description: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) has kept kerosene prices steady at Sh152.78 per litre in its most recent fuel price review, following a major government subsidy. According to EPRA...

item.title: Government explains extra loadshedding due to lower hydropower output
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The government on Wednesday issued an apology to electricity consumers for the unusually high load management, which exceeded the previously promised limits, due to lower water availability...

item.title: JD Vance faces sparse crowd and criticism over Trump and Iran war stance
item.description: Vice President JD Vance’s campus appearance in Georgia, hosted by the conservative group Turning Point USA, drew a sparse and uneasy crowd, highlighting the challenges faced by the Trump administratio...

item.title: Zanzibar to Invest in Seaweed Processing to Boost Farmers' Income
item.description: Zanzibar government is serious about boosting the seaweed sector, which is very important for coastal communities' livelihood. Deputy Minister for Blue Economy and Fisheries, Ms Mboja Ramadhan Mshenga...

item.title: PARI launches Awit Fest and Awit Museum to honor Original Pilipino Music
item.description: The Philippine Association of the Record Industry (PARI) has officially announced two major initiatives aimed at promoting and celebrating Original Pilipino Music (OPM). The first is the Awit Fest, a ...

item.title: DICT reminds citizens to report online misinformation promptly
item.description: The Department of Information and Communications Technology (DICT) has called on the public to take an active role in helping curb the spread of fake news by promptly reporting misleading or false pos...

item.title: Eduardo Oban Jr. appointed as new National Security Adviser after Eduardo Año’s retirement
item.description: Retired General Eduardo Oban Jr., former chief of staff of the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP), has been appointed as the country’s new National Security Adviser (NSA) following the retirement o...

item.title: Amit Shah promises focus on Gorkha issue in Darjeeling if BJP wins West Bengal
item.description: On April 15, 2026, Union Home Minister Amit Shah announced that resolving the Gorkha issue in Darjeeling would be a priority for the BJP if it assumes power in West Bengal. He also promised to withdra...

item.title: South African shops slow to lower food prices despite cheaper farm produce
item.description: In South Africa, despite a significant drop in the cost of producing staple foods, retail prices remain high, putting pressure on low-income households. The Competition Commission of South Africa repo...

item.title: Amazon invests $11.57 billion to strengthen its satellite internet project through Globalstar acquisition
item.description: Amazon has announced a massive investment of $11.57 billion (£8.5 billion) to purchase Globalstar, a satellite communications firm. The goal of this acquisition is to boost Amazon's long-term project ...

item.title: Hungarian Elections Bring New Leadership Amid Hopes and Uncertainties
item.description: There is widespread celebration in Hungary following the electoral defeat of long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, who has been a polarising figure in European politics for 16 years. His Fidesz pa...

item.title: Uber Plans Massive $10 Billion Investment in Robotaxi Fleets and Partnerships
item.description: Uber don decide to move away from im usual asset-light gig economy model to make sure dem no go fall behind for the robotaxi matter. Dem don commit over $10 billion to buy autonomous cars and take equ...

item.title: Geely E2: South Africa’s New Affordable Electric Option
item.description: Chinese automaker Geely has officially launched the E2 electric hatchback in South Africa, marking it as the country’s most affordable electric vehicle. Priced from R339,900 for the Aspire model, it u...
